title: Transactional Outbox, Inbox, and Sagas · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A multi-county incident response partnership. They need quorum safety, 4.3 retry and timeout policy, keyed ordering, stream selection, a cancellable outbox relay, duplicate-safe inbox effects, and compensated workflows with operator recovery. Planned artifact: Deliver Replicated Incident Workflow Platform with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Replica Majority Safety
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go messaging team handles case 4381 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Saga Compensation, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Saga Compensation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Saga Compensation, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A project Go messaging team handles case 6714 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Saga Compensation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A compensation always restores the world to its exact state before the original action. In Go, a goroutine that publishes after commit closes the dual-write failure window.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Project Saga Compensation
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go messaging team handles case 2139 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then fail the canary and prove older Go consumers remain compatible. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Project Saga Compensation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Project Saga Compensation, keep Saga Compensation connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Saga Compensation symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: RabbitMQ Reliability Guide — Controls failure ownership, reconnect, retransmission ambiguity, acknowledgements, confirms, replication, and reliable-delivery limits.

stimulus.title: Saga Compensation failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A compensation always restores the world to its exact state before the original action. In Go, a goroutine that publishes after commit closes the dual-write failure window.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Saga Compensation condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Saga Compensation happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Saga Compensation
steps.instruction:
Write the project evidence note for Saga Compensation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A project Go messaging team handles case 9520 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Saga Compensation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
